notes: v1.14.5 — landing globe graticule. The landing globe renders only real data (NASA night-lights, coastline/border geometry, CoreWeave hub arcs); the Pacific hemisphere is ~all ocean, so when it rotates front there were no points to draw and the disc read as an empty void (verified behavior, not a bug). Added a dim dotted lat/lon graticule so an empty face still reads as a sphere: ONE inserted IIFE between the border IIFE and the arc loop appends 732 points (12 meridians @30 deg lon, lat -75..75 step 5 deg; 5 parallels at -60/-30/0/30/60, lon -180..175 step 5 deg) to staticData in the standard 8-float vertex format (~23 KB). GRAT_R=0.998 seats it just under the lights (1.0) and borders (1.004); GRAT_COL=[0.13,0.18,0.34] dim slate-violet (dimmer than BORDER_COL); GRAT_SZ=0.8 (finer than borders' 1.3). Self-balances via the existing additive blend (SRC_ALPHA,ONE): swamped under the lit hemisphere, the only thing visible over dark ocean; the shader's front=smoothstep(0,0.3,z2) fades the back face so the far grid doesn't bleed through. SURGICAL: zero edits to existing geometry/shaders/buffers — staticArr/staticCount/upload pick up the appended points automatically. Verified: node --check 3/3 inline scripts + sw.js, block-0 CSS braces 2736/2736 (no CSS change), CRLF preserved (0 lone LF), four stamps lockstep v1.14.5, sw.js cache-bumped, git diff confirmed surgical (insert + 4 stamps only). NOT runtime-tested — WebGL can't render in the sandbox; John's on-device watch: (1) Pacific face now reads as a globe not a void, (2) grid stays quiet over the lit Americas/Atlantic and does not wash the SPECTRUM gradient/city lights, (3) over-sparkle (shared shader twinkle/flare) is a follow-up shader tweak if it shimmers, not this cut. WARNING: standing repo-only fixes — v1.13.8 (#boot full-bleed) and v1.14.4 (storage-full recovery) still lack a counterpart in the external/web-Claude build base; re-apply on the next import or they drop.
